content: When you are a baby, you are 75% water, but by adulthood, you are typically 50% to 55% water.
Electrolytes are minerals that help electrical charges within the body, aiding muscle contraction, nerve function, and mood regulation.
Electrolyte imbalances can lead to muscle cramps, fatigue, headaches, and irritability.
Consuming too much water can dilute electrolytes, leading to potential imbalances if kidneys cannot process excess water.
To avoid electrolyte imbalances, focus on a balanced intake of sodium, phosphate, calcium, magnesium, and potassium.
Whole foods and magnesium supplements are recommended for maintaining proper electrolyte levels and preventing overabundance of calcium.
